notes: Simulated the reported memory leak pattern (rapid surface recreation with SKPath drawing) in a console app on macOS arm64. Observed significant memory growth (~6x, +170-200 MB) that plateaus after ~250 iterations across all tested versions (2.88.9, 3.119.2, main). The growth pattern is consistent across versions, suggesting the core Skia memory behavior hasn't changed. The actual UWP-specific view-layer leak (SKXamlCanvas surface management during resize) cannot be verified without Windows — multiple users confirmed it was fixed in 3.116.1 via the view layer, not core Skia. Reporter's version 2.80.2 has no osx-arm64 native binary; 2.88.9 used as closest testable 2.x version.

reproductionSteps.description: Create console repro project with SkiaSharp 2.88.9 (reporter's 2.80.2 lacks arm64 native binary)
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/skiasharp/repro/1680 && cd /tmp/skiasharp/repro/1680 && dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 2.88.9
reproductionSteps.output: Restore succeeded. PackageReference for SkiaSharp 2.88.9 added.

versionResults.notes: Memory grew from 34 MB to 220 MB (~6x) then plateaued. Significant initial growth but not unbounded. Used 2.88.9 instead of reporter's 2.80.2 due to missing osx-arm64 native binary in 2.80.2.
Memory grew from 34 MB to 209 MB (~6x) then plateaued. Nearly identical pattern to 2.88.9. One notable difference: persistent surface test reclaims memory after dispose in 3.x (45 MB) vs 2.x (59 MB).
Memory grew from 34 MB to 219 MB (~6.4x) then plateaued. Same pattern as NuGet versions. Core Skia path drawing memory behavior unchanged on main.

blockers: Reporter's platform is UWP (Windows) — current environment is macOS arm64, UWP is not available
SkiaSharp 2.80.2 nupkg only contains x86_64 macOS native binary — no arm64 support, no Rosetta dotnet installed; used 2.88.9 as closest testable 2.x version
The reported fix in 3.116.1 appears to be in the UWP/WPF view layer (SKXamlCanvas surface lifecycle), not in core Skia — console simulation cannot reproduce view-layer behavior

corrections.upstream: Memory leak confirmed fixed in 3.116.1+ through Skia engine updates
corrections.corrected: Core Skia path/surface memory behavior is unchanged in 3.x. The fix was likely in the UWP/WPF view layer (surface lifecycle management during resize), not in core Skia. Console simulation shows identical ~6x memory growth across all versions.

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for the report. We attempted to reproduce this by simulating rapid surface recreation with SKPath drawing in a console application on macOS.

**What we observed:**
- Working set memory grows significantly (~6x, from ~34 MB to ~220 MB) during rapid surface creation/destruction with path drawing
- This growth **plateaus** and does not grow unboundedly
- The pattern is consistent across SkiaSharp 2.88.9, 3.119.2, and the main branch

**What we couldn't test:**
- The UWP-specific behavior with `SKXamlCanvas` and window resize events (requires Windows)
- SkiaSharp 2.80.2 (no macOS arm64 native binary available)

Multiple users in #2368 have confirmed that upgrading to SkiaSharp 3.116.1+ resolved their memory leak issues. The fix appears to be in the view layer's surface lifecycle management during resize events, rather than in core Skia.

Since UWP support was dropped in SkiaSharp 3.x, migration to WinUI would be needed to pick up this fix. If you're still actively affected, could you share whether migration to WinUI + SkiaSharp 3.x is feasible for your project?

actionability.reason: Console simulation shows significant but bounded memory growth (~6x) across all versions. The actual UWP view-layer leak cannot be tested on macOS. Multiple users in #2368 confirmed 3.116.1 fixed their issue. The OP is on UWP which is not supported in 3.x, so we cannot ask them to verify. Need confirmation whether the view-layer fix in 3.x resolved the reporter's specific scenario.

workarounds: Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.116.1+ (requires migrating from UWP to WinUI)
Debounce/throttle resize events to reduce surface recreation frequency

actions.description: Cross-reference #2368 which tracks the same root cause with more investigation

missingInfo: Whether the reporter can migrate from UWP to WinUI to test SkiaSharp 3.x
Windows host with UWP development tools for direct reproduction of the view-layer leak
